name: Valle Navaluenga to the Embalse del Burguillo — circular by the Río Alberche
name_original: Valle Navaluenga hasta el Embalse del Burguillo — circular por el Río Alberche
region_intro: Hiking around Navaluenga offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Gredos, characterized by its prominent mountain range and the Alberche River. The region features riverside paths, tracks through agricultural fields, and routes winding through pine forests and oak groves. Notable natural features include the Burguillo Reservoir and several gorges, providing varied landscapes for exploration. Best hiking trails around Navaluenga The most popular hiking route is Valle Navaluenga to the Embalse del Burguillo — circular by the Río Alberche, an 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete, following the Alberche River to the Burguillo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Navaluenga Valley loop from Navaluenga, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding valley and takes approximately 2 hours 17 minutes. Local hikers also love the Puente Viejo de Navaluenga loop from Navaluenga, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through riverside areas and past the historic Puente Viejo, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Navaluenga is defined by the Sierra de Gredos foothills, the Alberche River, and diverse pine and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate climbs. The routes in Navaluenga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Navaluenga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Navaluenga?
A: Hiking trails in Navaluenga offer a diverse landscape, ranging from riverside paths along the Alberche River to tracks through agricultural fields and meadows. You'll also find routes winding through lush pine forests and oak groves, with the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra de Gredos. Some areas, like the Granitic Massif of Gredos, present rugged and inaccessible terrain for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Navaluenga offers several easy routes. For a gentle stroll, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar El Seco – Puente Viejo de Navaluenga loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 7 km trail. Many riverside paths also provide accessible options for a relaxed walk.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Navaluenga?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails around Navaluenga are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145159848/valle-navaluenga-to-the-embalse-del-burguillo-circular-by-the-rio-alberche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle Navaluenga to the Embalse del Burguillo — circular by the Río Alberche&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along the river to the reservoir and back.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Navaluenga?
A: Navaluenga is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4268792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viejo de Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features impressive gorges like Garganta de Lanchamala and the valuable forest of Garganta del Barranco Cambronal.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Navaluenga?
A: Yes, many trails in Navaluenga are dog-friendly, allowing your companions to enjoy the natural surroundings with you. It's always a good idea to keep dogs on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock, and to respect local regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navaluenga?
A: The autumn months are particularly recommended for hiking in Navaluenga due to the vibrant transformation of the landscape, offering stunning colors. Spring also provides pleasant temperatures and blooming flora. Summers can be warm, but riverside paths offer cooler options, and winters are generally mild enough for hiking, especially at lower elevations.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or water activities after a hike?
A: Yes, the Alberche River is a central feature of Navaluenga and offers natural pools for swimming during the summer months. You can also find opportunities for water sports like rafting, kayaking, and canoeing towards the Burguillo Reservoir.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navaluenga?
A: The hiking routes in Navaluenga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful riverside sections, and the stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos and the Burguillo Reservoir.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Navaluenga area?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes available around Navaluenga, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include easy riverside strolls and more moderate climbs into the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any challenging hiking routes for experienced hikers in Navaluenga?
A: While many routes are moderate, the Sierra de Gredos, which borders Navaluenga, offers more challenging mountaineering opportunities. Areas like the Granitic Massif of Gredos, specifically the Garganta de Peñaltar, are recommended for experienced hikers due to their rugged and inaccessible terrain.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views while hiking in Navaluenga?
A: For wide panoramic views, consider routes that ascend towards mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Casillas&lt;/a&gt;, one of the main entrances to the Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve, offers expansive vistas on clear days. The renowned &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Mijares&lt;/a&gt; also provides scenic beauty, though it's often associated with cycling.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Navaluenga?
A: Many of the easier and moderate routes along the Alberche River or through the pine forests are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Valley loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.8 km path, offers pleasant views and is a good option for families looking for a slightly longer walk.
tips: This old stone bridge is located in a beautiful setting in the town of Navaluenga. The bridge allows you to cross the Alberche River and divides the two bathing areas of the Navaluenga natural pools during the summer months. An ideal place to add to my route.
Beautiful views that I find when I reach this point. A natural viewpoint of the Alberche river that is undoubtedly worth visiting.

Following the Río Alberche and from the town of Navaluenga, this valley appears, on the south side we find the Sierra de Gredos and to the north, the Sierra de Panamera that offers this green and wide valley.
